mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum; stronger demand translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quintile RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which weakens aggregate consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and an increase in unemployment capture a joint supply-demand contraction that amplifies equity downside risk beyond either signal in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE produces lower directional accuracy than the best-performing single-series signal over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 50% in joint-signal months.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained elevated federal funds rate increases discount rates and corporate debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods identified as monetary tightening cycles, the level of FEDFUNDS fails to show negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns, with Rank IC exceeding -0.02 or positive returns occurring more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and construction activity, a leading component of aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quintile HOUST growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Negative month-over-month payroll employment growth signals outright job losses and contraction in aggregate wage income, leading to reduced consumption and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS month-over-month change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates improving manufacturing utilization and operating leverage, which boosts cyclical corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quintile INDPRO growth months.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sharp deceleration in real GDP growth to below 1% quarter-over-quarter annualized signals exhaustion of demand and impending earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansion periods, GDPC1 growth below 1% quarter-over-quarter annualized fails to predict negative SPX returns, with positive 20-day returns exceeding 50% or Rank IC above -0.02.
